upior.sandwraith.weaknessDetail: The Sand Wraith Power Core is exposed during its charge attack animation. This 3-second window is the only time you can deal significant damage. The core is located on its back, requiring flanking or a coordinated distraction.
upior.sandwraith.strategy: Never engage a Sand Wraith head-on. Assign one crew member as bait to trigger its charge while the rest flank to attack the Power Core. Bring Railguns for maximum damage during the vulnerability window. If you lack heavy weapons, retreat and regroup.
upior.dunestalker.weaknessDetail: Exposed joints on all four limbs can be targeted to cripple the Dune Stalker movement. Destroying both front joints permanently reduces its speed by 60%, making it vulnerable to ranged attacks.
upior.dunestalker.strategy: Focus fire on the joints first to immobilize the Dune Stalker. A stationary Dune Stalker is far less dangerous. Use Rifles for precision joint shots, then close in with Shotguns once it is slowed. Avoid standing directly in front of it.
upior.dustcrawler.weaknessDetail: The Dust Crawler head has a glowing weak point that pulses before it attacks. Aiming for this spot deals triple damage and can stagger the creature, interrupting its attack.
upior.dustcrawler.strategy: Wait for the head pulse animation before firing for maximum damage. The Dust Crawler telegraphs its attacks, giving you time to line up headshots. Keep medium distance.
upior.scrapgolem.weaknessDetail: Back plates can be pried off with melee attacks, revealing the internal mechanisms. Once exposed, the Scrap Golem takes 50% more damage from all sources. Flanking is essential.
upior.scrapgolem.strategy: Send one player around the back to pry off plates while others distract from the front. Once plates are removed, everyone should focus fire on the exposed mechanisms. Heavy Cannons excel at breaking through the remaining armor.
upior.sandswarm.weaknessDetail: Fire and explosive weapons are the only effective counter. Individual swarm members are immune to single-target damage. Area-of-effect attacks hit multiple swarm members simultaneously.
upior.sandswarm.strategy: Equip Leviathan and Heavy Cannon before engaging. Create denial zones that the swarm must pass through, then bombard with explosives. Never use single-target weapons.
upior.rustlurker.weaknessDetail: No specific weakness. Any weapon is effective. Rust Lurkers are low-threat enemies designed for new players to practice combat. Focus on headshots for quick takedowns.
upior.rustlurker.strategy: Treat Rust Lurkers as target practice. Use them to warm up your aim at the start of a session. They are great for testing new weapons and practicing headshots. No special strategy needed.
tramplers_intro: Tramplers are the walking mechs of SAND: Raiders of Sophie, your crew primary vehicle for exploration, combat, and extraction. Each Trampler class offers distinct advantages: Light frames for speed and recon, Medium for balanced operations, and Heavy for siege and transport. Choose wisely based on your crew size, mission type, and preferred playstyle.
weapons_intro: Your arsenal in SAND: Raiders of Sophie splits between Mounted weapons fitted to Tramplers and Player-carried weapons used by crew members. Each weapon type excels in different scenarios, from the railgun armor-piercing precision to the shotgun devastating close-range blasts. Matching the right weapon to the right situation is key to survival.
upiors_intro: Upiors are the hostile entities roaming the desert, from the terrifying Sand Wraith to the lowly Rust Lurker. Each Upior type has unique attack patterns, weaknesses, and threat levels. Understanding these enemies is essential for planning your approach, selecting the right weapons, and making it back to extraction alive.
tierList_intro: Our SAND: Raiders of Sophie tier list ranks all Tramplers and weapons based on combat effectiveness, versatility, survivability, build cost, and crew synergy. Rankings are updated regularly based on community data, developer patches, and competitive play trends.
gameModes_intro: SAND: Raiders of Sophie features two core game modes, each offering a fundamentally different experience. Voyage mode is a cooperative PvE sandbox, while Storm Dive adds the tension of PvPvE competition. Understanding both modes is essential for getting the most out of the game.
codes_intro: Promo codes in SAND: Raiders of Sophie provide free resources, building materials, and research supplies. New codes are released regularly through Discord, Twitter, and community events. Check back often since some codes expire within 24 hours.
tierList_weapon_tierS_desc: Must-have weapons that dominate in every game mode and crew composition
tierList_weapon_tierA_desc: Strong weapons that are excellent in the right hands and crew setup
tierList_weapon_tierB_desc: Solid weapons that are good for mid-game and specific roles
tierList_weapon_tierC_desc: Niche weapons that are useful in limited situations or as backups
